possible_choices: X and Y are uncorrelated and independent.
X and Y are uncorrelated and dependent.
X and Y are correlated and independent.
X and Y are correlated and dependent.
choice_explanations: Consider the deifnition of independence at x = -0.8. Is the conditional distribution for y given x = -0.8 the same as the marginal distribution for y?
This would have been the correct answer.
Consider the definition of correlation. Is there a monotonic relationship between the two variables?
Consider the definition of correlation. Is there a monotonic relationship between the two variables? Consider the definition of dependence. For which x value would P(y|x) ever be unequal p(y)?
